ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for avocado coffee. FOODON_03301776 (coffee beverage) is the closest defensible parent class. Avocado blending component is not captured in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (coffee-based beverage) is the closest defensible match. No specific code exists for avocado-blended coffee in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for avocado coffee beverage. SNOMED CT does not maintain a specific concept for this preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Avocado Coffee is approximately 350 g (one full cup or glass), which is the typical portion size served at Vietnamese cafes and food delivery outlets.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Avocado, raw (FDC ID 171705) and Coffee, brewed (FDC ID 171890) and Sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 172187); composite estimation for avocado coffee beverage
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Vietnamese avocado coffee (Cà Phê Bơ) as a composite beverage. Nutrient values were estimated by compositing USDA FoodData Central data for raw avocado (~40% by weight), brewed coffee (~45% by weight), and sweetened condensed milk (~15% by weight), consistent with standard Vietnamese café preparation ratios. Input per_100g values (energy 100 kcal, protein 1.43 g, fat 5.71 g, carbohydrate 11.43 g, sugar 8.57 g, sodium 22.86 mg, dietary fibre 1.43 g, calcium 28.57 mg, iron 0.29 mg, cholesterol 5.71 mg, saturated fat 2.29 g, trans fat 0.06 g, vitamin C 1.43 mg) are consistent with this composite and are retained. Additional micronutrients estimated from USDA component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on avocado (GI ~15) and sweetened condensed milk coffee composite
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Vietnamese avocado coffee (Cà Phê Bơ). GI estimated at approximately 40 (low category) based on: avocado GI ~15 (University of Sydney GI Database; avocado's high fat and fibre content strongly attenuates glycaemic response), sweetened condensed milk GI ~61 (University of Sydney GI Database), and brewed coffee GI ~0 (negligible carbohydrate). Weighted composite at approximate preparation ratios (avocado 40%, coffee 45%, condensed milk 15%) yields estimated GI ~40. Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (40 × 11.43 × 3.5) / 100 = 16.0 g. This is a conservative estimate; actual GI may be lower due to avocado fat and fibre blunting glucose absorption.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.57 g sugar per 100 g from sweetened condensed milk; sodium is low at 22.86 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Although sodium is very low (22.86 mg/100 g), the high sugar content (8.57 g/100 g) from sweetened condensed milk classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage, which independently raises blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention per AHA 2018 guidelines; patients with hypertension should request an unsweetened version using black coffee blended with plain avocado only.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage threshold exceeded: 8.57 g sugar per 100 g from added condensed milk and sugar; liquid sugar causes rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: The 8.57 g/100 g sugar content from sweetened condensed milk triggers the sugar-sweetened beverage hard gate for Type 2 Diabetes management; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this preparation and instead request an unsweetened version with black coffee and plain avocado, which would provide beneficial MUFA and dietary fibre without the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 2.29 g/100 g from condensed milk; avocado provides cardioprotective MUFA (estimated 2.10 g/100 g); high sugar load from condensed milk raises triglyceride risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Avocado contributes cardioprotective monounsaturated fatty acids and potassium, but the sweetened condensed milk adds saturated fat (2.29 g/100 g) and a high sugar load (8.57 g/100 g) that may raise triglycerides and LDL; patients with cardiovascular disease should request an unsweetened preparation to preserve the beneficial lipid profile of avocado without the atherogenic sugar and saturated fat contribution of condensed milk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar (8.57 g/100 g) from condensed milk promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; avocado MUFA and coffee polyphenols provide partial anti-inflammatory offset
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The refined sugar from sweetened condensed milk (8.57 g/100 g) promotes inflammatory cytokine production and is not offset by avocado's anti-inflammatory MUFA or coffee's chlorogenic acid polyphenols at this sugar concentration; patients with rheumatoid arthritis should eliminate condensed milk and added sugar to convert this into an anti-inflammatory beverage.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Avocado Coffee (Vietnamese: Cà Phê Bơ, romanized: Ca Phe Bo) is a popular Vietnamese beverage made by blending ripe avocado with strong brewed Vietnamese coffee, typically sweetened with sweetened condensed milk and sometimes additional sugar. The drink originated in Vietnam and has become widely available across Southeast Asia, including Singapore, through café menus and food delivery platforms. The preparation involves blending fresh avocado flesh with chilled or room-temperature brewed coffee and condensed milk until smooth and creamy, producing a thick, velvety texture with a rich, buttery mouthfeel and a bittersweet coffee flavour balanced by the natural creaminess of avocado. Per 100 g, the beverage provides approximately 100 kcal, 1.43 g protein, 5.71 g fat (of which 2.29 g is saturated fat and approximately 2.10 g is monounsaturated fat from avocado), 11.43 g carbohydrate, and 8.57 g sugar predominantly from condensed milk. Dietary fibre is 1.43 g/100 g, contributed by avocado. Sodium is low at 22.86 mg/100 g. A standard serving of 350 g delivers approximately 350 kcal and 40 g of carbohydrate with a glycemic load of approximately 16 g. Clinically, this beverage is classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage due to its sugar content exceeding 5 g/100 g, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension in its standard sweetened form. The avocado component contributes beneficial MUFA and dietary fibre, which partially attenuate the glycaemic response, but cannot offset the condensed milk sugar load. An unsweetened version using black coffee and plain avocado would be substantially more suitable for metabolic health conditions. The beverage is vegetarian and gluten-free but contains dairy from condensed milk.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Vietnamese avocado coffee as a composite beverage. Nutrient values were estimated by compositing USDA FoodData Central data for raw avocado, brewed coffee, and sweetened condensed milk at standard preparation ratios. The input per_100g values from the GrabFood-sourced record are consistent with this composite and have been retained. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for raw avocado (FDC ID 171705), brewed coffee (FDC ID 171890), and sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 172187) at approximate preparation ratios of 40% avocado, 45% brewed coffee, and 15% condensed milk. The input per_100g values from the GrabFood-sourced record are consistent with this composite and have been retained as the primary values. 2. Fields set to -1 (data not available): vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — detailed B-vitamin, fat-soluble vitamin, and trace mineral data for this composite beverage preparation are not directly available in USDA FoodData Central as a single composite entry; individual component data would require weighted blending beyond the scope of this record. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.10 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.65 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central avocado fatty acid profile at the 40% avocado ratio. 4. potassium_mg (152.0 mg) and vitamin_a_mcg_rae (5.0 mg) are estimated from USDA component data at preparation ratios. 5. water_g (78.0 g) is estimated from the composite preparation. 6. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Cà Phê Bơ exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 7. The original input source listed FAO/INFOODS SEA as supporting only 'menu_listing'; nutrient values have been re-sourced to USDA FoodData Central as the approved primary source for this composite. 8. trans_fat_g is 0.06 g/100 g, a trace amount consistent with dairy-derived trans fats from condensed milk (vaccenic acid); this is not an industrial trans fat concern at this level.
